# Qualia Expands Product Development Team for New Product Launches

We have expanded our product development team significantly. The move coincides with consumer demand for new formulations in the wake of our [Qualia nootropic](https://www.qualialife.com/qualia-mind) product line's success. We expect to accelerate product development substantially, releasing several significant products over the next year. The team expansion will enable the development of solutions for healthy aging, sleep, anxiety, pain, mood, metabolic health, fitness performance, immune support, and more.

Notable additions and changes to the team include Shawn Ramer, PhD; Gregory Kelly, ND; and Sara Adães, PhD.

[Shawn Ramer, PhD](https://www.linkedin.com/in/shawn-ramer/) joins as SVP of Product Development. Ramer most recently served as SVP of Information Technology for the global pharmaceutical company, Bristol-Myers Squibb. At BMS, Ramer held several roles that combined his scientific, R&D and IT expertise. Prior to Bristol-Myers Squibb, Ramer held roles in chemistry, R&D, and information management for Hoffmann-La Roche, a global pioneer in pharmaceuticals and diagnostics. Ramer earned his PhD in organic chemistry from the University of Alberta, and completed post-doc work in bioorganic chemistry at Harvard Medical School. He has now joined Qualia in an on-going role as SVP of Product Development.

[Gregory Kelly, ND](https://www.linkedin.com/in/gregory-kelly-98b96b138/) signs on as Lead Product Formulator, another major coup for the company. Selected from hundreds of applicants, Dr. Gregory Kelly is a naturopathic doctor and subject matter expert on nutrition, wellness, and preventive medicine. Some of his recent groundbreaking research is featured in his book, [Shape Shift: The Shape Intelligence Solution](https://www.amazon.com/Shape-Shift-Intelligence-Solution-ebook/dp/B0711S1JSC/ref=sr_1_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1531580513&sr=8-2&keywords=gregory+kelly). His solution-minded approach to problems and broad scope of experience, including clinical practice, nutraceutical research and formulation, and medical publications position him perfectly for his role as Qualia's Lead Product Formulator.

[Sara Adães, PhD](https://www.linkedin.com/in/saradaes/) is a neuroscience researcher; her work has included research in neuropharmacology, neurobiology of pain, and inflammation and neuropathy caused by osteoarthritis. She has a Licenciate's Diploma in Biochemistry and earned her PhD in Neuroscience at the Faculty of Medicine of the University of Porto, in Portugal. Adães has been working part-time for Qualia since 2016, and will now be expanding her research role, developing new product formulations.

"The Qualia product development philosophy is deeply focused on the complex systems modeling necessary to deliver real efficacy at scale in the nutraceutical space," says CEO, James Schmachtenberger. "We look to provide people with the products, resources, and tools they need to help their whole being—mind, brain, and body—work together in harmony. Our expanding product development team comprised of doctors, neuroscientists, and chemists have identified product opportunities where we can use our unique formulation process to deliver the best product in the market for each category. As our company grows and the science evolves we are committed to continuing to develop best in class products to enhance well-being."

For press release: <https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/neurohacker-expands-product-development-team-for-new-product-launches-300684470.html>

# Empathy: The Catalyst For Global Change | James Schmachtenberger

![Image 3](https://cdn1.neurohacker.com/Y4LvgUgMLQ2ZVtFYuhNQpizD.jpg) Written by [Tina Gammon](https://www.qualialife.com/people/staffwriters)

![Image 4: Empathy: The Catalyst For Global Change | James Schmachtenberger](https://cdn1.neurohacker.com/zxyxS7Bb1zD4WbKe2JGFXSV5.png)

The problems facing the world are too vast and complex to be solved solely by innovation or technology. Though both are critical, they aren't sufficient without being informed and ultimately driven by a deeper understanding of how to support all life.

This talk explores the role empathy plays in upgrading our underlying value systems, allowing us to leverage technology and innovation to create a world that supports a thriving of all life. James Schmachtenberger, CEO at Qualia, is a San Diego area tech and wellness entrepreneur, filmmaker, political activist and former director of a holistic health college. James' broad and accomplished background stems from a unique education emphasizing the interconnected nature of large scale problems. This has driven James his entire life to approach societal challenges with solutions that account for the entirety of a problem rather than solving for one issue while creating another.

This talk was given at a TEDx event using the TED conference format but independently organized by a local community. Learn more at[https://www.ted.com/tedx](https://www.ted.com/about/programs-initiatives/tedx-program)

# Deep Sleep - Dr. Dan Pardi - Neuroscience

![Image 3](https://cdn1.neurohacker.com/CfjoZpemg2rGwSdyHhoBVAHv) Written by [Daniel Schmachtenberger](https://qualialife.com/people/daniel-schmachtenberger)

![Image 4: Deep Sleep - Dr. Dan Pardi - Neuroscience](https://cdn1.neurohacker.com/P82RPfyEqrF6v739f4bcFQH7.jpg)

If there was ever an ultimate sleep episode, **this is it.**

Dan Pardi, sleep researcher at Stanford and Leiden University and CEO of humanOS.me, an application designed to promote health mastery, is here to unpack the exact science of deep sleep. And even better, _how to get more of it_.

We discuss sleep stages, sleep physiology, the major causes of sleep loss in our society (think high reward activities and mistimed lighting environments) and the consequences of sleep loss, including decreased purging of toxic substances from the brain and damage to neurons that signal to the brain to stay awake. To be honest, that description barely scratches the surface. We also cover:

*   The physiological benefits of slow wave sleep
*   How sleep impacts eating behaviors and weight gain
*   The optimal amount of sleep and the importance of sleep timing
*   How sleep regulates the immune system and effects [cellular senescence](https://www.qualialife.com/the-formulator-s-view-of-the-qualia-senolytic-ingredients)
*   Effects of light on circadian rhythms
*   Science-backed ways to optimize your “master clock”
*   Dan’s favorite routines and products to biohack your sleep

> There’s no part of the body that goes untouched when we don’t get the sleep that we need. It is a physiological requirement. - Dan Pardi, Ph.D.

If you’re looking to get the most from your sleep, this episode is for you. If you want even more after listening you’re in luck. This is part one of a two part series. Listen in now.

[![Image 5](https://cdn1.neurohacker.com/GgWj71RZsVLCcqNCQiFnbhUz.png)](https://www.qualialife.com/the-neuroscience-of-deep-sleep-what-it-is-and-how-to-get-more-of-it)

# Dan Pardi, MS, PhD

Chief Health Officer

Dr. Dan Pardi is the **Chief Health Officer at Qualia Life Science****s,** where he leads health education, protocol design, and health-improvement strategies. His work centers on translating cutting-edge science into actionable information and programs that support healthspan optimization and peak performance.

Previously, Dan founded [humanOS.me](https://humanos.me/), a digital health platform developed with over 100 health-science professors. Its podcast, [humanOS Radio](https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/humanos-radio/id1115847173), became the official podcast of the Sleep Research Society and a content partner of the Buck Institute for Research on Aging. He also served as Chief Health Architect at Restore Hyper Wellness, where he led new product strategy and served as key authority for intervention sciences.

Through Vivendi Health, Dan provides bespoke consulting to individuals and organizations facing unique health challenges. He holds a PhD in Cognitive Neuroscience from Leiden University and Stanford, and a Master’s in Exercise Physiology from Florida State. He’s spoken at TEDx, the Institute for Human Machine Cognition, among others.

## How to Keep Your Joints Young and Strong for Life

[Joint health supplements](https://www.qualialife.com/joint-supplements) play a crucial role in maintaining mobility and helping to alleviate joint discomfort as we age. In this episode, Dr. Gregory Kelly, a naturopathic doctor, senior researcher, and head of research and formulation at [Qualia Life Sciences](https://www.qualialife.com/), joins Qualia CEO James Schmachtenberger to explore the latest science behind joint health supplements. They debunk common misconceptions, highlight proactive strategies for long-term joint support, and dive into cutting-edge research on key ingredients. Dr. Kelly shares insights on innovative compounds like Ovomet and CuberUp, which are revolutionizing the way we approach joint care and mobility support.*

**“If we don't love our joints early they won’t be there to save us when we're older." - Greg Kelly, ND**

Beyond supplements, we discuss the gut-joint axis, cellular energy’s role in joint function, and why traditional approaches often fall short. Dr. Kelly shares how mitochondrial dysfunction accelerates joint degeneration and highlights the overlooked connection between [gut health](https://www.qualialife.com/magnesium-for-gut-health) and joint wellness. Listeners will gain valuable insights into why joint health supplements should be part of a holistic lifestyle that includes proper nutrition, movement, and targeted supplementation.*

Occasional joint pain and stiffness may seem inevitable, but they don’t have to be. Dr. Kelly explains how proactive joint care—including high-quality joint health supplements, strategic movement, and proper nutrition—can help mitigate damage before it starts. He highlights the importance of ingredients like [L-carnitine](https://www.qualialife.com/l-carnitine-benefits-uses-and-sources) and how they contribute to joint resilience at the cellular level.*

We also introduce [Qualia Joint Health](https://www.qualialife.com/shop/qualia-joint), a cutting-edge supplement designed to support tendons and cartilage with scientifically-backed ingredients. Unlike conventional joint supplements, which focus solely on glucosamine and chondroitin, Qualia Joint Health incorporates novel compounds sourced from upcycled materials to enhance absorption and effectiveness.*

**Key Takeaways:**

*   **The gut-joint connection:** A healthy gut microbiome plays a crucial role in regulating joint inflammation and maintaining long-term mobility.*

*   **Beyond glucosamine:**Traditional supplements aren’t enough—ingredients like [Boswellia](https://www.qualialife.com/boswellia-benefits), Ovomet and CuberUp offer new possibilities for joint health.*

*   **Mitochondrial support matters:** Cellular energy is critical for joint function, and certain nutrients can enhance this process.*

*   **Proactive care is key:** Waiting until your joints start slowing you down may limit your options—early support leads to better outcomes.*

*   **Holistic approaches work best:** Combining the right supplements with diet, movement, and gut health strategies leads to the best results.*

As we age, it’s not just about avoiding pain—it’s about maintaining the ability to move freely and do the things we love. Whether you’re an athlete, an active professional, or someone simply looking to stay mobile, understanding the science behind joint health supplements can empower you to make informed choices. This episode provides practical, science-based solutions to help you stay ahead of joint deterioration and live a more active, comfortable life.

Tune in to learn how joint health supplements, combined with lifestyle changes, can help you take control of your mobility and well-being for years to come.*

Prefer to listen on YouTube? Bookmark [Greg Kelly, ND: How to Keep Your Joints Young and Strong for Life](https://youtu.be/DHwP_mdCgxo) and listen later!

# Magnesium - Dr. Greg Kelly - Bioavailability

![Image 3](https://cdn1.neurohacker.com/ydJDAzVCKgLHTdhtBBeCevrN.png) Written by [James Schmachtenberger](https://www.qualialife.com/people/staffwriters)

![Image 4: Magnesium - Dr. Greg Kelly - Bioavailability ](https://cdn1.neurohacker.com/w5ds6nnbyhc0yc3x6374ymd6qxzy.jpg)

## Do You Need More Magnesium? Exploring Benefits and Bioavailability

In this episode we discuss the [critical role of magnesium](https://www.qualialife.com/why-do-we-need-magnesium) in overall health, longevity, and brain function. [Magnesium](https://www.qualialife.com/benefits-of-magnesium-glycinate) is crucial for over 600 enzyme reactions in the body, significantly impacting brain health and all twelve hallmarks of aging. Unfortunately, widespread magnesium deficiencies affect 50–80% of the population, impeding optimal health, yet this crucial issue often goes unnoticed. This in-depth conversation with Dr. Greg Kelly and James Schmachtenberger explores the wide-ranging benefits, common deficiencies, and innovative approaches to magnesium supplementation.

Dr. Gregory Kelly, head of research at Qualia, emphasizes magnesium's essential functions, noting, "It's used in 600 plus enzymes." This mineral supports critical processes such as DNA repair, mitochondrial function, and ATP production. As Dr. Kelly remarked, “For ATP to do its job, which is essentially to fuel our cells, it must be always bound to magnesium.” This relationship highlights how crucial magnesium is for cellular energy and resilience. They further discuss the numerous forms of magnesium, their bioavailability, and the unique blend used in Qualia's new [magnesium supplement](https://www.qualialife.com/shop/qualia-magnesium) to optimize tissue retention and overall effectiveness.*

**"Magnesium directly touches on the two things that we're most interested in at Qualia, brain health and longevity." -Greg Kelly, ND**

#### Key Takeaways:

*   **Magnesium's Importance:**Essential for over 600 enzymatic reactions, brain health, and resistance to stress--highlighted for [magnesium's pivotal role in brain health, longevity](https://www.qualialife.com/cutting-edge-science-why-magnesium-is-crucial-for-brain-health).

*   **Common Deficiencies:** 50-80% of the U.S. population has inadequate [magnesium levels](https://www.qualialife.com/10-magnesium-rich-foods-for-your-diet) due to dietary and lifestyle factors.

*   **Symptoms and Benefits:** Mood swings, sleep issues, muscle cramps, and GI problems are common signs of deficiency.

*   **Forms and Bioavailability:**Discussion on various forms of magnesium, their bioavailability, and the innovative formula used in Qualia Magnesium+.

*   **Trace Minerals:** The importance of combining magnesium with 70 trace minerals to mimic natural sources and enhance overall health.*

_For deeper insights and to uncover more details on magnesium, listen to the full episode:_

However, many individuals remain unaware of their magnesium levels. Symptoms like mood swings, sleep disturbances, and gastrointestinal issues can significantly affect quality of life. Dr. Kelly notes that stress exacerbates magnesium deficiency, creating a vicious cycle. "If stress is high, that's almost always going to be a clue that you should up your magnesium intake," he advises.*

Moreover, different forms of magnesium have unique benefits. For instance, magnesium acetyl taurate excels at boosting brain magnesium, promoting mental calmness, while magnesium malate is praised for bioavailability but may not significantly support muscle or brain magnesium. By combining various types of magnesium, individuals can optimize their benefits. Dr. Kelly explains, “We have a variety of nine different forms, all of which are bioavailable, retained well, and shown to enhance tissue levels.”*

These insights have practical implications for personal health choices. Studies indicate magnesium's potential to maintain healthy brain tissue and support cognitive function. A recent study involving over 6,000 participants found that those with higher magnesium intake had more brain volume and fewer lesions, emphasizing magnesium's role in neuroprotection.

Additionally, magnesium aspartate is noted for its benefits in hearing health, especially in a society increasingly exposed to noise-induced stress from headphone use. Dr. Kelly states, "Magnesium plays a prominent role in helping hearing resist noise stress, and it's the best-researched nutrient in this capacity."

With its profound implications for brain health and longevity, magnesium should be prioritized in daily supplementation. Understanding its wide-reaching benefits—from muscle function to emotional balance—underlines magnesium’s necessity in our health routines.*

By reframing common beliefs about nutrients and intelligently designing supplements that complement natural patterns of intake, we can bring substantial benefits. As our understanding of magnesium deepens through innovative research, optimizing magnesium intake may become a cornerstone of proactive health management, guiding both individual habits and future innovations in wellness.*

For those interested in exploring magnesium further, check out these resources: [Qualia Magnesium Ingredients](https://www.qualialife.com/qualia-magnesium-ingredients) provides an overview of the various forms of magnesium included in their supplements. Learn about the impact of magnesium on aging in [Magnesium and Aging](https://www.qualialife.com/magnesium-aging). Finally, delve into the [Cutting Edge Science: Why Magnesium is Crucial for Brain Health](https://www.qualialife.com/cutting-edge-science-why-magnesium-is-crucial-for-brain-health) to understand its vital role in cognitive function.

Prefer to listen on YouTube? Bookmark [Greg Kelly, ND: Magnesium for Brain Health and Longevity](https://youtu.be/O435BvaAgvA) and listen later!

Resources:

[Study about magnesium amounts dropping in produce](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7649274/)

[Blog with 2023 study on magnesium and brain size](https://www.qualialife.com/cutting-edge-science-why-magnesium-is-crucial-for-brain-health?srsltid=AfmBOopx9aA6gCENZj7LQL8FCT6JXH1rWYkLqVogyRVBxjQGmZ4HtXRs)

## The Science of Magnesium, Magnified

It’s not just the amount of magnesium that goes in your mouth that matters: it’s what gets to your cells. Some forms of magnesium do better than others when it comes to optimizing magnesium in different tissues. We selected the ten forms of magnesium and 70+ minerals found in QUALIA MAGNESIUM+ with this idea in mind*. Experience the science of magnesium, magnified. # Neurohacker Collective Is Becoming Qualia Life Sciences

![Image 3](https://cdn1.neurohacker.com/Y4LvgUgMLQ2ZVtFYuhNQpizD.jpg) Written by [Tina Gammon](https://www.qualialife.com/people/staffwriters) , reviewed by [Dr. Gregory Kelly](https://www.qualialife.com/people/greg-kelly)

![Image 4: Neurohacker Collective Is Becoming Qualia Life Sciences](https://cdn1.neurohacker.com/8qudexnf2gqp6nhh7nrj12v1yxw5.jpg)

Neurohacker Collective is becoming Qualia Life Sciences.

Our [Qualia supplement](https://www.qualialife.com/shop) line blends ingredients to complement each other for a targeted net effect within the body.

Since 2016, Neurohacker Collective has been releasing supplement products under the Qualia brand name. "The name Neurohacker Collective made sense when we focused solely on brain health and mental performance products, but our continued growth and expansion into longevity and other high-growth categories now makes that name selection too limiting", said CEO James Schmachtenberger. As such, the company will start operating under the DBA Qualia Life Sciences. The company's ecommerce store will be hosted on qualialife.com.

"Our science and advisory team has grown in recent years, allowing us to broaden our scope of formulation. We're now formulating products with as much emphasis on life sciences beyond neuroscience as within it."

The word [qualia](https://www.qualialife.com/) is a philosophical term meaning "the experience of being oneself", such as the uniqueness of one person experiencing the color red compared to another person.

"The idea behind the Qualia brand was always to empower the unique potential of each human experience, and with our broadening health focuses, the name captures that intent perfectly." adds Schmachtenberger.

The health formulas created by Qualia Life Sciences often combine a multitude of ingredients to complement each other for a targeted net effect within the human body. This formulation method supports balanced regulatory systems within the human body to enhance health, rather than overriding the body's regulatory systems to achieve a short-sighted effect.

Their holistic approach has won praise from naturopathic communities and mainstream health influencers such as Ben Greenfield, Dave Asprey, Dr. Molly Maloof, and more.

"We're excited our approach is resulting in product requests across a large spectrum of health needs," adds Schmachtenberger. "Qualia Life Sciences is up for that challenge."

## About Qualia Life Sciences

Qualia Life Sciences was established in 2015 to enhance the quality of life through cutting-edge wellbeing formulations. Their science team emphasizes the principles of naturopathy and has pioneered formulation techniques using complex systems science, which recognizes the body's inherent self-regulation capacity as a key factor in addressing various health issues. Their expanding product lineup addresses a wide variety of health needs, such as [aging](https://www.qualialife.com/shop/qualia-life), [brain health](https://www.qualialife.com/shop/qualia-mind), [senescent cells](https://www.qualialife.com/shop/qualia-senolytic), [sleep](https://www.qualialife.com/shop/qualia-night), [skin](https://www.qualialife.com/shop/qualia-skin), and energy, with many products under development.
